---
title: "halfnormal_prior"
generated: true
description: "Build a half-normal lengthscale prior that penalises large `rho`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.priors`](../nitrix-stats-priors.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:halfnormal_prior -->
```python
halfnormal_prior(sd: float) -> PriorFn
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:halfnormal_prior -->
Build a half-normal lengthscale prior that penalises large `rho`.

The prior density is
$p(\rho) \propto \exp(-\rho^2 / (2\,\sigma^2))$ on $\rho > 0$,
so the returned penalty is
$-\log p(\rho) = \rho^2 / (2\,\sigma^2)$ (up to an additive
constant): a soft cap that pulls the estimate down, away from over-smooth
(near-linear) fits.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:halfnormal_prior -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `sd` | `float` | — | — | The half-normal scale $\sigma$ (must be positive). Smaller `sd` is a stronger pull toward small $\rho$; a sensible value is on the order of the covariate range. |

**Returns** `PriorFn`. A callable mapping a scalar lengthscale $\rho$ (shape `()`) to the scalar negative log-density $-\log p(\rho)$ (shape `()`), suitable for the `map_rho=` argument of [`nitrix.stats.gp_fit`](../nitrix-stats-gp/gp_fit.qmd) and [`nitrix.stats.hgp_fit`](../nitrix-stats-hgp/hgp_fit.qmd).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
